### ✅ `github-mcp-server-get_file_contents`

**/infrastructure/infrastructure-setup-bicep/15-private-network-standard-agent-setup/modules-network-secured/private-endpoint-and-dns.bicep**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "owner": "microsoft-foundry",
  "repo": "foundry-samples",
  "path": "/infrastructure/infrastructure-setup-bicep/15-private-network-standard-agent-setup/modules-network-secured/private-endpoint-and-dns.bicep"
}
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>358 lines</summary>

```
successfully downloaded text file (SHA: a73c59468d9b84262783b2c584e73c428fa46f7e)/*
Private Endpoint and DNS Configuration Module
------------------------------------------
This module configures private network access for Azure services using:

1. Private Endpoints:
   - Creates network interfaces in the specified subnet
   - Establishes private connections to Azure services
   - Enables secure access without public internet exposure

2. Private DNS Zones:
   - Enables custom DNS resolution for private endpoints

3. DNS Zone Links:
   - Links private DNS zones to the VNet
   - Enables name resolution for resources in the VNet
   - Prevents DNS resolution conflicts

Security Benefits:
- Eliminates public internet exposure
- Enables secure access from within VNet
- Prevents data exfiltration through network
*/

// Resource names and identifiers
@description('Name of the AI Foundry account')
param aiAccountName string
@description('Name of the AI Search service')
param aiSearchName string
@description('Name of the storage account')
param storageName string
@description('Name of the Cosmos DB account')
param cosmosDBName string
@description('Name of the Vnet')
param vnetName string
@description('Name of the Customer subnet')
param peSubnetName string
@description('Suffix for unique resource names')
param suffix string

@description('Resource Group name for existing Virtual Network (if different from current resource group)')
param vnetResourceGroupName string = resourceGroup().name

@description('Subscription ID for Virtual Network')
param vnetSubscriptionId string = subscription().subscriptionId

@description('Resource Group name for Storage Account')
param storageAccountResourceGroupName string = resourceGroup().name

@description('Subscription ID for Storage account')
param storageAccountSubscriptionId string = subscription().subscriptionId

@description('Subscription ID for AI Search service')
param aiSearchSubscriptionId string = subscription().subscriptionId

@description('Resource Group name for AI Search service')
param aiSearchResourceGroupName string = resourceGroup().name

@description('Subscription ID for Cosmos DB account')
param cosmosDBSubscriptionId string = subscription().subscriptionId

@description('Resource group name for Cosmos DB account')
param cosmosDBResourceGroupName string = resourceGroup().name

@description('Map of DNS zone FQDNs to resource group names. If provided, reference existing DNS zones in this resource group instead of creating them.')
param existingDnsZones object = {
  'privatelink.services.ai.azure.com': ''
  'privatelink.openai.azure.com': ''
  'privatelink.cognitiveservices.azure.com': ''
  'privatelink.search.windows.net': ''
  'privatelink.blob.${environment().suffixes.storage}': ''
  'privatelink.documents.azure.com': ''
}

@description('Subscription ID where existing private DNS zones are located. Should be resolved to current subscription if empty.')
param dnsZonesSubscriptionId string

// ---- Resource references ----
resource aiAccount 'Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts@2023-05-01' existing = {
  name: aiAccountName
  scope: resourceGroup()
}

resource aiSearch 'Microsoft.Search/searchServices@2023-11-01' existing = {
  name: aiSearchName
  scope: resourceGroup(aiSearchSubscriptionId, aiSearchResourceGroupName)
}

resource storageAccount 'Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts@2023-05-01' existing = {
  name: storageName
  scope: resourceGroup(storageAccountSubscriptionId, storageAccountResourceGroupName)
}

resource cosmosDBAccount 'Microsoft.DocumentDB/databaseAccounts@2024-11-15' existing = {
  name: cosmosDBName
  scope: resourceGroup(cosmosDBSubscriptionId, cosmosDBResourceGroupName)
}

// Reference existing network resources
resource vnet 'Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks@2024-05-01' existing = {
  name: vnetName
  scope: resourceGroup(vnetSubscriptionId, vnetResourceGroupName)
}
resource peSubnet 'Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets@2024-05-01' existing = {
  parent: vnet
  name: peSubnetName
}

/* -------------------------------------------- AI Foundry Account Private Endpoint -------------------------------------------- */

// Private endpoint for AI Services account
// - Creates network interface in customer hub subnet
// - Establishes private connection to AI Services account
resource aiAccountPrivateEndpoint 'Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints@2024-05-01' = {
  name: '${aiAccountName}-private-endpoint'
  location: resourceGroup().location
  properties: {
    subnet: { id: peSubnet.id } // Deploy in customer hub subnet
    privateLinkServiceConnections: [
      {
        name: '${aiAccountName}-private-link-service-connection'
        properties: {
          privateLinkServiceId: aiAccount.id
          groupIds: [ 'account' ] // Target AI Services account
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}

/* -------------------------------------------- AI Search Private Endpoint -------------------------------------------- */

// Private endpoint for AI Search
// - Creates network interface in customer hub subnet
// - Establishes private connection to AI Search service
resource aiSearchPrivateEndpoint 'Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints@2024-05-01' = {
  name: '${aiSearchName}-private-endpoint'
  location: resourceGroup().location
  properties: {
    subnet: { id: peSubnet.id } // Deploy in customer hub subnet
    privateLinkServiceConnections: [
      {
        name: '${aiSearchName}-private-link-service-connection'
        properties: {
          privateLinkServiceId: aiSearch.id
          groupIds: [ 'searchService' ] // Target search service
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}

/* -------------------------------------------- Storage Private Endpoint -------------------------------------------- */

// Private endpoint for Storage Account
// - Creates network interface in customer hub subnet
// - Establishes private connection to blob storage
resource storagePrivateEndpoint 'Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints@2024-05-01' = {
  name: '${storageName}-private-endpoint'
  location: resourceGroup().location
  properties: {
    subnet: { id: peSubnet.id } // Deploy in customer hub subnet
    privateLinkServiceConnections: [
      {
        name: '${storageName}-private-link-service-connection'
        properties: {
          privateLinkServiceId: storageAccount.id // Target blob storage
          groupIds: [ 'blob' ]
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}

/*--------------------------------------------- Cosmos DB Private Endpoint -------------------------------------*/

resource cosmosDBPrivateEndpoint 'Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints@2024-05-01' = {
  name: '${cosmosDBName}-private-endpoint'
  location: resourceGroup().location
  properties: {
    subnet: { id: peSubnet.id } // Deploy in customer hub subnet
    privateLinkServiceConnections: [
      {
        name: '${cosmosDBName}-private-link-service-connection'
        properties: {
          privateLinkServiceId: cosmosDBAccount.id // Target Cosmos DB account
          groupIds: [ 'Sql' ]
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}

/* -------------------------------------------- Private DNS Zones -------------------------------------------- */

// Format: 1) Private DNS Zone
//         2) Link Private DNS Zone to VNet
//         3) Create DNS Zone Group for Private Endpoint

// Private DNS Zone for AI Services (Account)
// 1) Enables custom DNS resolution for AI Services private endpoint

var aiServicesDnsZoneName = 'privatelink.services.ai.azure.com'
var openAiDnsZoneName = 'privatelink.openai.azure.com'
var cognitiveServicesDnsZoneName = 'privatelink.cognitiveservices.azure.com'
var aiSearchDnsZoneName = 'privatelink.search.windows.net'
var storageDnsZoneName = 'privatelink.blob.${environment().suffixes.storage}'
var cosmosDBDnsZoneName = 'privatelink.documents.azure.com'

// ---- DNS Zone Resource Group lookups ----
var aiServicesDnsZoneRG = existingDnsZones[aiServicesDnsZoneName]
var openAiDnsZoneRG = existingDnsZones[openAiDnsZoneName]
var cognitiveServicesDnsZoneRG = existingDnsZones[cognitiveServicesDnsZoneName]
var aiSearchDnsZoneRG = existingDnsZones[aiSearchDnsZoneName]
var storageDnsZoneRG = existingDnsZones[storageDnsZoneName]
var cosmosDBDnsZoneRG = existingDnsZones[cosmosDBDnsZoneName]

// ---- DNS Zone Resources and References ----
resource aiServicesPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' = if (empty(aiServicesDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: aiServicesDnsZoneName
  location: 'global'
}

// Reference existing private DNS zone if provided
resource existingAiServicesPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' existing = if (!empty(aiServicesDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: aiServicesDnsZoneName
  scope: resourceGroup(dnsZonesSubscriptionId, aiServicesDnsZoneRG)
}
//creating condition if user pass existing dns zones or not
var aiServicesDnsZoneId = empty(aiServicesDnsZoneRG) ? aiServicesPrivateDnsZone.id : existingAiServicesPrivateDnsZone.id

resource openAiPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' = if (empty(openAiDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: openAiDnsZoneName
  location: 'global'
}

// Reference existing private DNS zone if provided
resource existingOpenAiPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' existing = if (!empty(openAiDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: openAiDnsZoneName
  scope: resourceGroup(dnsZonesSubscriptionId, openAiDnsZoneRG)
}
//creating condition if user pass existing dns zones or not
var openAiDnsZoneId = empty(openAiDnsZoneRG) ? openAiPrivateDnsZone.id : existingOpenAiPrivateDnsZone.id

resource cognitiveServicesPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' = if (empty(cognitiveServicesDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: cognitiveServicesDnsZoneName
  location: 'global'
}

// Reference existing private DNS zone if provided
resource existingCognitiveServicesPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' existing = if (!empty(cognitiveServicesDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: cognitiveServicesDnsZoneName
  scope: resourceGroup(dnsZonesSubscriptionId, cognitiveServicesDnsZoneRG)
}
//creating condition if user pass existing dns zones or not
var cognitiveServicesDnsZoneId = empty(cognitiveServicesDnsZoneRG) ? cognitiveServicesPrivateDnsZone.id : existingCognitiveServicesPrivateDnsZone.id

resource aiSearchPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' = if (empty(aiSearchDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: aiSearchDnsZoneName
  location: 'global'
}

// Reference existing private DNS zone if provided
resource existingAiSearchPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' existing = if (!empty(aiSearchDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: aiSearchDnsZoneName
  scope: resourceGroup(dnsZonesSubscriptionId, aiSearchDnsZoneRG)
}
//creating condition if user pass existing dns zones or not
var aiSearchDnsZoneId = empty(aiSearchDnsZoneRG) ? aiSearchPrivateDnsZone.id : existingAiSearchPrivateDnsZone.id

resource storagePrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' = if (empty(storageDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: storageDnsZoneName
  location: 'global'
}

// Reference existing private DNS zone if provided
resource existingStoragePrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' existing = if (!empty(storageDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: storageDnsZoneName
  scope: resourceGroup(dnsZonesSubscriptionId, storageDnsZoneRG)
}
//creating condition if user pass existing dns zones or not
var storageDnsZoneId = empty(storageDnsZoneRG) ? storagePrivateDnsZone.id : existingStoragePrivateDnsZone.id

resource cosmosDBPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' = if (empty(cosmosDBDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: cosmosDBDnsZoneName
  location: 'global'
}

// Reference existing private DNS zone if provided
resource existingCosmosDBPrivateDnsZone 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones@2020-06-01' existing = if (!empty(cosmosDBDnsZoneRG)) {
  name: cosmosDBDnsZoneName
  scope: resourceGroup(dnsZonesSubscriptionId, cosmosDBDnsZoneRG)
}
//creating condition if user pass existing dns zones or not
var cosmosDBDnsZoneId = empty(cosmosDBDnsZoneRG) ? cosmosDBPrivateDnsZone.id : existingCosmosDBPrivateDnsZone.id

// ---- DNS VNet Links ----
resource aiServicesLink 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/virtualNetworkLinks@2024-06-01' = if (empty(aiServicesDnsZoneRG)) {
  parent: aiServicesPrivateDnsZone
  location: 'global'
  name: 'aiServices-${suffix}-link'
  properties: {
    virtualNetwork: { id: vnet.id }
    registrationEnabled: false
  }
}
resource openAiLink 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/virtualNetworkLinks@2024-06-01' = if (empty(openAiDnsZoneRG)) {
  parent: openAiPrivateDnsZone
  location: 'global'
  name: 'aiServicesOpenAI-${suffix}-link'
  properties: {
    virtualNetwork: { id: vnet.id }
    registrationEnabled: false
  }
}
resource cognitiveServicesLink 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/virtualNetworkLinks@2024-06-01' = if (empty(cognitiveServicesDnsZoneRG)) {
  parent: cognitiveServicesPrivateDnsZone
  location: 'global'
  name: 'aiServicesCognitiveServices-${suffix}-link'
  properties: {
    virtualNetwork: { id: vnet.id }
    registrationEnabled: false
  }
}
resource aiSearchLink 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/virtualNetworkLinks@2024-06-01' = if (empty(aiSearchDnsZoneRG)) {
  parent: aiSearchPrivateDnsZone
  location: 'global'
  name: 'aiSearch-${suffix}-link'
  properties: {
    virtualNetwork: { id: vnet.id }
    registrationEnabled: false
  }
}
resource storageLink 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/virtualNetworkLinks@2024-06-01' = if (empty(storageDnsZoneRG)) {
  parent: storagePrivateDnsZone
  location: 'global'
  name: 'storage-${suffix}-link'
  properties: {
    virtualNetwork: { id: vnet.id }
    registrationEnabled: false
  }
}
resource cosmosDBLink 'Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/virtualNetworkLinks@2024-06-01' = if (empty(cosmosDBDnsZoneRG)) {
  parent: cosmosDBPrivateDnsZone
  location: 'global'
  name: 'cosmosDB-${suffix}-link'
  properties: {
    virtualNetwork: { id: vnet.id }
    registrationEnabled: false
  }
}

// ---- DNS Zone Groups ----
resource aiServicesDnsGroup 'Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints/privateDnsZoneGroups@2024-05-01' = {
  parent: aiAccountPrivateEndpoint
  name: '${aiAccountName}-dns-group'
  properties: {
    privateDnsZoneConfigs: [
      { name: '${aiAccountName}-dns-aiserv-config', properties: { privateDnsZoneId: aiServicesDnsZoneId } }
      { name: '${aiAccountName}-dns-openai-config', properties: { privateDnsZoneId: openAiDnsZoneId } }
      { name: '${aiAccountName}-dns-cogserv-config', properties: { privateDnsZoneId: cognitiveServicesDnsZoneId } }
    ]
  }
  dependsOn: [
    empty(aiServicesDnsZoneRG) ? aiServicesLink : null
    empty(openAiDnsZoneRG) ? openAiLink : null
    empty(cognitiveServicesDnsZoneRG) ? cognitiveServicesLink : null
  ]
}
resource aiSearchDnsGroup 'Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints/privateDnsZoneGroups@2024-05-01' = {
  parent: aiSearchPrivateEndpoint
  name: '${aiSearchName}-dns-group'
  properties: {
    privateDnsZoneConfigs: [
      { name: '${aiSearchName}-dns-config', properties: { privateDnsZoneId: aiSearchDnsZoneId } }
    ]
  }
  dependsOn: [
    empty(aiSearchDnsZoneRG) ? aiSearchLink : null
  ]
}
resource storageDnsGroup 'Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints/privateDnsZoneGroups@2024-05-01' = {
  parent: storagePrivateEndpoint
  name: '${storageName}-dns-group'
  properties: {
    privateDnsZoneConfigs: [
      { name: '${storageName}-dns-config', properties: { privateDnsZoneId: storageDnsZoneId } }
    ]
  }
  dependsOn: [
    empty(storageDnsZoneRG) ? storageLink : null
  ]
}
resource cosmosDBDnsGroup 'Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints/privateDnsZoneGroups@2024-05-01' = {
  parent: cosmosDBPrivateEndpoint
  name: '${cosmosDBName}-dns-group'
  properties: {
    privateDnsZoneConfigs: [
      { name: '${cosmosDBName}-dns-config', properties: { privateDnsZoneId: cosmosDBDnsZoneId } }
    ]
  }
  dependsOn: [
    empty(cosmosDBDnsZoneRG) ? cosmosDBLink : null
  ]
}
```

</details>

**/infrastructure/infrastructure-setup-bicep/15-private-network-standard-agent-setup/modules-network-secured/network-agent-vnet.bicep**

  "owner": "microsoft-foundry",
  "repo": "foundry-samples",
  "path": "/infrastructure/infrastructure-setup-bicep/15-private-network-standard-agent-setup/modules-network-secured/network-agent-vnet.bicep"
}

<details>
<summary>55 lines</summary>

```
successfully downloaded text file (SHA: bad8a4f27d36aa34e8ba9a37c61a1050195f933d)@description('Azure region for the deployment')
param location string

@description('The name of the virtual network')
param vnetName string

@description('Indicates if an existing VNet should be used')
param useExistingVnet bool = false

@description('Subscription ID of the existing VNet (if different from current subscription)')
param existingVnetSubscriptionId string = subscription().subscriptionId

@description('Resource Group name of the existing VNet (if different from current resource group)')
param existingVnetResourceGroupName string = resourceGroup().name

@description('The name of Agents Subnet')
param agentSubnetName string = 'agent-subnet'

@description('The name of Private Endpoint subnet')
param peSubnetName string = 'pe-subnet'

@description('Address space for the VNet (only used for new VNet)')
param vnetAddressPrefix string = ''

@description('Address prefix for the agent subnet')
param agentSubnetPrefix string = ''

@description('Address prefix for the private endpoint subnet')
param peSubnetPrefix string = ''

// Create new VNet if needed
module newVNet 'vnet.bicep' = if (!useExistingVnet) {
  name: 'vnet-deployment'
  params: {
    location: location
    vnetName: vnetName
    agentSubnetName: agentSubnetName
    peSubnetName: peSubnetName
    vnetAddressPrefix: vnetAddressPrefix
    agentSubnetPrefix: agentSubnetPrefix
    peSubnetPrefix: peSubnetPrefix
  }
}

// Use existing VNet if requested
module existingVNet 'existing-vnet.bicep' = if (useExistingVnet) {
  name: 'existing-vnet-deployment'
  params: {
    vnetName: vnetName
    vnetResourceGroupName: existingVnetResourceGroupName
    vnetSubscriptionId: existingVnetSubscriptionId
    agentSubnetName: agentSubnetName
    peSubnetName: peSubnetName
    agentSubnetPrefix: agentSubnetPrefix
    peSubnetPrefix: peSubnetPrefix
  }
}

// Provide unified outputs regardless of which module was used
output virtualNetworkName string = useExistingVnet ? existingVNet.outputs.virtualNetworkName : newVNet.outputs.virtualNetworkName
output virtualNetworkId string = useExistingVnet ? existingVNet.outputs.virtualNetworkId : newVNet.outputs.virtualNetworkId
output virtualNetworkSubscriptionId string = useExistingVnet ? existingVNet.outputs.virtualNetworkSubscriptionId : newVNet.outputs.virtualNetworkSubscriptionId
output virtualNetworkResourceGroup string = useExistingVnet ? existingVNet.outputs.virtualNetworkResourceGroup : newVNet.outputs.virtualNetworkResourceGroup
output agentSubnetName string = agentSubnetName
output peSubnetName string = peSubnetName
output agentSubnetId string = useExistingVnet ? existingVNet.outputs.agentSubnetId : newVNet.outputs.agentSubnetId
output peSubnetId string = useExistingVnet ? existingVNet.outputs.peSubnetId : newVNet.outputs.peSubnetId
```

</details>

successfully downloaded text file (SHA: 90aebfbd30f0be3dfd3ceedbf6dc20a49d8f84db)param accountName string
param location string
param projectName string
param projectDescription string
param displayName string

param aiSearchName string
param aiSearchServiceResourceGroupName string
param aiSearchServiceSubscriptionId string

param cosmosDBName string
param cosmosDBSubscriptionId string
param cosmosDBResourceGroupName string

param azureStorageName string
param azureStorageSubscriptionId string
param azureStorageResourceGroupName string

resource searchService 'Microsoft.Search/searchServices@2024-06-01-preview' existing = {
  name: aiSearchName
  scope: resourceGroup(aiSearchServiceSubscriptionId, aiSearchServiceResourceGroupName)
}
resource cosmosDBAccount 'Microsoft.DocumentDB/databaseAccounts@2024-12-01-preview' existing = {
  name: cosmosDBName
  scope: resourceGroup(cosmosDBSubscriptionId, cosmosDBResourceGroupName)
}
resource storageAccount 'Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts@2023-05-01' existing = {
  name: azureStorageName
  scope: resourceGroup(azureStorageSubscriptionId, azureStorageResourceGroupName)
}

resource account 'Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts@2025-04-01-preview' existing = {
  name: accountName
  scope: resourceGroup()
}

resource project 'Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/projects@2025-04-01-preview' = {
  parent: account
  name: projectName
  location: location
  identity: {
    type: 'SystemAssigned'
  }
  properties: {
    description: projectDescription
    displayName: displayName
  }

  resource project_connection_cosmosdb_account 'connections@2025-04-01-preview' = {
    name: cosmosDBName
    properties: {
      category: 'CosmosDB'
      target: cosmosDBAccount.properties.documentEndpoint
      authType: 'AAD'
      metadata: {
        ApiType: 'Azure'
        ResourceId: cosmosDBAccount.id
        location: cosmosDBAccount.location
      }
    }
  }

  resource project_connection_azure_storage 'connections@2025-04-01-preview' = {
    name: azureStorageName
    properties: {
      category: 'AzureStorageAccount'
      target: storageAccount.properties.primaryEndpoints.blob
      authType: 'AAD'
      metadata: {
        ApiType: 'Azure'
        ResourceId: storageAccount.id
        location: storageAccount.location
      }
    }
  }

  resource project_connection_azureai_search 'connections@2025-04-01-preview' = {
    name: aiSearchName
    properties: {
      category: 'CognitiveSearch'
      target: 'https://${aiSearchName}.search.windows.net'
      authType: 'AAD'
      metadata: {
        ApiType: 'Azure'
        ResourceId: searchService.id
        location: searchService.location
      }
    }
  }

}

output projectName string = project.name
output projectId string = project.id
output projectPrincipalId string = project.identity.principalId

#disable-next-line BCP053
output projectWorkspaceId string = project.properties.internalId

// return the BYO connection names
output cosmosDBConnection string = cosmosDBName
output azureStorageConnection string = azureStorageName
output aiSearchConnection string = aiSearchName
```

**Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways bicep sku property location 2024-05-01**

  "query": "Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways bicep sku property location 2024-05-01"

````
{"type":"output_text","text":{"value":"For the Bicep representation of the `Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways` resource, as of the 2025-05-01 (and similar for 2024-05-01) API version, the `sku` property must be provided within the `properties` object of the resource. The structure is as follows:\n\n```bicep\nresource virtualNetworkGateway 'Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways@2025-05-01' = {\n  name: 'your-gateway-name'\n  location: 'your-location'\n  properties: {\n    ...\n    sku: {\n      name: 'string' // example: 'VpnGw1', 'HighPerformance', etc.\n      tier: 'string' // must match or be appropriate for the sku name\n    }\n    ...\n  }\n}\n```\n\nThe `sku` block is **nested inside the `properties` section**, not a top-level property, and it requires both `name` and `tier` fields as strings. This format is referenced directly from the official Microsoft documentation for Bicep templates and Azure Resource Manager (ARM) templates for this resource type and API version【3:0†source】.\n\nYou must also supply the `location` property at the top level of the resource, which specifies the Azure region of the gateway.\n\n**Summary of required structure:**\n- `location` is a top-level property (outside `properties`).\n- `sku` is nested under `properties`.\n\nFor officially current property references and allowed values in each SKU, always consult the [Microsoft Learn documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.network/virtualnetworkgateways) for your specific API version.","annotations":[{"text":"","start_index":942,"end_index":954,"url_citation":{"title":"Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways - Bicep, ARM template ...","url":"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.network/virtualnetworkgateways"}}]},"bing_searches":[{"text":"Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways bicep sku property location 2024-05-01","url":"https://www.bing.com/search?q=Microsoft.Network%2FvirtualNetworkGateways+bicep+sku+property+location+2024-05-01"}],"annotations":null}
````

**ARM template Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways 2024-05-01 sku top level or properties**

  "query": "ARM template Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways 2024-05-01 sku top level or properties"
}
```

</details>

````
{"type":"output_text","text":{"value":"In Azure Resource Manager (ARM) templates, the **sku** property for the resource type `Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways` (as of API version `2024-05-01`) is **defined as a property inside `properties`**, not as a top-level property.\n\nHere is the correct structure excerpt for an ARM template defining a Virtual Network Gateway:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways\",\n  \"apiVersion\": \"2024-05-01\",\n  \"name\": \"<gateway-name>\",\n  \"location\": \"<location>\",\n  \"properties\": {\n    \"sku\": {\n      \"name\": \"VpnGw1\",\n      \"tier\": \"VpnGw1\"\n    },\n    // ...other properties like gatewayType, vpnType, etc.\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Explanation\n\n- The **sku** block contains **`name`** and (**sometimes**) **`tier`**.\n- It is **nested under `properties`, not at the resource’s top level**.\n\n### Reference\n\n[Microsoft documentation for Virtual Network Gateways](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.network/virtualnetworkgateways?pivots=deployment-language-arm-template) confirms this structure for recent API versions, including `2024-05-01`.\n\nLet me know if you need an example template or further details!","annotations":null},"bing_searches":null,"annotations":null}
````

}","issue_events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/issues/events{/number}","issues_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/issues{/number}","keys_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/keys{/key_id}","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/labels{/name}","languages_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/languages","merges_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/merges","milestones_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/milestones{/number}","notifications_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/notifications{?since,all,participating}","pulls_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/pulls{/number}","releases_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/releases{/id}","stargazers_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/stargazers","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/statuses/{sha}","subscribers_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/subscribers","subscription_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/subscription","tags_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/tags","trees_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/git/trees{/sha}","teams_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/teams"}}]}
```


### 💬 Copilot

Overall: **subnet sizing/layout, the four core private endpoints, and the DNS zone-group mappings are conceptually correct**, but there are **multiple blocking gaps** that mean this is **not yet a valid Foundry standard/BYO VNet deployment**.

## Blocking Issues

1. **Bicep does not compile**
   - **Where:** `main.bicep:586`
   - **Issue:** `sku: { name: 'VpnGw1' tier: 'VpnGw1' }` is missing a comma/newline.
   - **Impact:** Deployment cannot start.
   - **Fix:** Correct the `sku` object formatting.

2. **BYO VNet is modeled, but not actually attached to Foundry**
   - **Where:** `main.bicep:216-233`
   - **Issue:** The Foundry account lacks the `networkInjections` configuration that binds the delegated agent subnet, and it also omits `allowProjectManagement`.
   - **Impact:** You created the right subnets, but the account is not configured for secured standard-agent network injection.
   - **Fix:** Add the same pattern used in Microsoft’s sample: `networkInjections` with `scenario: 'agent'`, `subnetArmId`, `useMicrosoftManagedNetwork: false`, plus `allowProjectManagement: true`.

3. **Critical Foundry agent resources are missing**
   - **Where:** `main.bicep:258-268` and entire template
   - **Issue:** The template creates the project, but **does not create project connections** (Storage/Search/Cosmos) and **does not create a project capability host**.
   - **Impact:** Standard agent setup will not be usable even if infra deploys; agent state/resource wiring is incomplete.
   - **Fix:** Add:
     - project `connections` for Storage, AI Search, Cosmos DB
     - `Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/projects/capabilityHosts@2025-04-01-preview`

4. **RBAC is assigned to the wrong principal and with the wrong roles**
   - **Where:** `main.bicep:423-474`
   - **Issue:** Roles are assigned to `aiServices.identity.principalId` (account MI), but standard setup uses the **project managed identity**. Also the role set does not match Microsoft guidance:
     - missing **Storage Account Contributor**
     - missing **Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor** SQL role assignment at DB scope after capability host
     - using **Storage Blob Data Owner** at account scope and **Storage Queue Data Contributor**, which is not the documented pattern
   - **Impact:** Capability host provisioning and runtime access are likely to fail with 403/authorization errors.
   - **Fix:** Follow the sample/doc flow:
     - pre-cap-host: project MI gets **Cosmos DB Operator** + **Storage Account Contributor**
     - search: **Search Index Data Contributor** + **Search Service Contributor**
     - post-cap-host: container/database-scoped blob/cosmos data roles

5. **Preflight provider/permission validation is incomplete**
   - **Where:** `test-config.json:51-60`, `00-preflight.ps1:79-95`
   - **Issue:** Required providers are missing/misaligned with docs:
     - missing `Microsoft.DocumentDB`
     - missing `Microsoft.MachineLearningServices`
     - extra `Microsoft.ApiManagement` appears unrelated to deployed infra
     - permission check only looks for Owner/Contributor, not the documented role-assignment capability / Azure AI account owner requirements
   - **Impact:** Preflight can pass while deployment later fails.
   - **Fix:** Update provider list and validate for role assignment capability (`Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write`) / documented roles.

## Non-Blocking Issues

1. **APIM config/test drift**
   - **Where:** `test-config.json:17-20, 33-49`, `10-test-portal-access.ps1:125-137`
   - **Issue:** Config includes APIM subnet, APIM DNS zone (`privatelink.azure-api.net`), and ApiManagement provider, but `main.bicep` deploys none of that.
   - **Impact:** The portal test will produce false failures for DNS links.
   - **Fix:** Remove APIM from config/tests or actually deploy APIM.

2. **Portal/VPN test script does not validate the things it claims**
   - **Where:** `10-test-portal-access.ps1`
   - **Issue:** It checks existence/state, but not:
     - actual `ai.azure.com` reachability from the jumpbox
     - DNS resolution from inside the VNet
     - AAD tenant/audience/issuer correctness for P2S
   - **Impact:** False confidence.
   - **Fix:** Add explicit checks for VPN AAD fields and real in-VNet resolution/connectivity steps.

3. **Bastion/VM deployment can fail silently if password env var is unset**
   - **Where:** `main.bicepparam:20`
   - **Issue:** `readEnvironmentVariable('VM_ADMIN_PASSWORD', '')` can pass an empty password while `deployBastion=true`.
   - **Impact:** Deployment failure at VM creation time.
   - **Fix:** Fail fast in preflight if Bastion is enabled and the env var is empty.

## Suggestions

1. **Use cloud-aware endpoints**
   - **Where:** `main.bicep:57`, `main.bicep:602`
   - **Fix:** Use `environment().suffixes.storage` and avoid hardcoded public-cloud login URLs unless this is intentionally Azure Public only.

2. **Avoid array-index DNS coupling**
   - **Where:** `main.bicep:352-407`
   - **Fix:** Map DNS zones by name instead of relying on `dnsZones[0..5]`.

## Direct answers to your checklist

- **1. Bicep correctness:** **No** — currently fails compilation.
- **2. BYO VNet architecture:** **Subnet sizes/layout are correct**, but the Foundry account is **not wired to the delegated subnet**.
- **3. Private Endpoints:** **Yes**, the 4 expected PEs are present.
- **4. DNS Zone Groups:** **Mappings look correct** for those 4 PEs.
- **5. RBAC:** **No** — principal and role model do not match Microsoft’s standard-agent setup.
- **6. Bastion + VM:** **Mostly reasonable**, but password handling is weak.
- **7. VPN Gateway P2S:** **AAD values are in the right public-cloud pattern**, but template currently doesn’t compile and test script doesn’t validate them.
- **8. Missing items:** **Yes** — most importantly **network injection, project connections, and capability host**.

1. // ============================================================================
2. // Foundry BYO VNet - Main Bicep Template
3. // Deploys: VNet, Subnets, Foundry Account, Project, PE, DNS, RBAC
4. // Based on: microsoft-foundry/foundry-samples/15-private-network-standard-agent-setup
5. // ============================================================================
6. 
7. @description('Azure region for all resources')
8. param location string = 'swedencentral'
9. 
10. @description('Foundry account name prefix')
11. param aiServicesName string = 'ai-foundry-byovnet'
12. 
13. @description('Model deployment configuration')
14. param modelName string = 'gpt-4.1'
15. param modelFormat string = 'OpenAI'
16. param modelVersion string = '2025-04-14'
17. param modelSkuName string = 'GlobalStandard'
18. param modelCapacity int = 30
19. 
20. @description('Project configuration')
21. param projectName string = 'project-agent-test'
22. param projectDescription string = 'Foundry Agent with BYO VNet network isolation test'
23. 
24. @description('VNet configuration')
25. param vnetName string = 'vnet-foundry-test'
26. param vnetAddressPrefix string = '192.168.0.0/16'
27. param agentSubnetName string = 'snet-agent'
28. param agentSubnetPrefix string = '192.168.0.0/24'
29. param peSubnetName string = 'snet-pe'
30. param peSubnetPrefix string = '192.168.1.0/24'
31. 
32. @description('Bastion + Jumpbox VM for portal access testing')
33. param deployBastion bool = true
34. param vmAdminUsername string = 'azureadmin'
35. @secure()
36. param vmAdminPassword string
37. 
38. @description('VPN P2S Gateway for client remote access')
39. param deployVpnGateway bool = true
40. 
41. @description('Unique suffix for resource names')
42. param uniqueSuffix string = uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)
43. 
44. // ============================================================================
45. // Variables
46. // ============================================================================
47. var aiServicesUniqueName = '${aiServicesName}-${uniqueSuffix}'
48. var storageAccountName = 'stfoundry${uniqueSuffix}'
49. var searchServiceName = 'srch-foundry-${uniqueSuffix}'
50. var cosmosAccountName = 'cosmos-foundry-${uniqueSuffix}'
```

</details>

608. 
609. // ============================================================================
610. // Azure Bastion + Jumpbox VM (for portal access testing)
611. // ============================================================================
612. resource bastionPip 'Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses@2024-05-01' = if (deployBastion) {
613.   name: 'pip-bastion'
614.   location: location
615.   sku: { name: 'Standard' }
616.   properties: {
617.     publicIPAllocationMethod: 'Static'
618.   }
619. }
620. 
621. resource bastion 'Microsoft.Network/bastionHosts@2024-05-01' = if (deployBastion) {
622.   name: 'bastion-foundry-test'
623.   location: location
624.   sku: { name: 'Standard' }
625.   properties: {
626.     ipConfigurations: [
627.       {
628.         name: 'bastionIpConfig'
629.         properties: {
630.           publicIPAddress: { id: bastionPip.id }
631.           subnet: { id: vnet.properties.subnets[2].id } // AzureBastionSubnet
632.         }
633.       }
634.     ]
635.   }
636. }
637. 
638. resource jumpboxNic 'Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces@2024-05-01' = if (deployBastion) {
639.   name: 'nic-jumpbox'
640.   location: location
641.   properties: {
642.     ipConfigurations: [
643.       {
644.         name: 'ipconfig1'
645.         properties: {
646.           privateIPAllocationMethod: 'Dynamic'
647.           subnet: { id: vnet.properties.subnets[3].id } // snet-mgmt
648.         }
649.       }
650.     ]
651.   }
652. }
653. 
654. resource jumpboxVm 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines@2024-07-01' = if (deployBastion) {
655.   name: 'vm-jumpbox'
656.   location: location
657.   properties: {
658.     hardwareProfile: { vmSize: 'Standard_B2s' }
659.     osProfile: {
660.       computerName: 'jumpbox'
661.       adminUsername: vmAdminUsername
662.       adminPassword: vmAdminPassword
663.     }
664.     storageProfile: {
665.       imageReference: {
666.         publisher: 'MicrosoftWindowsDesktop'
667.         offer: 'windows-11'
668.         sku: 'win11-24h2-pro'
669.         version: 'latest'
670.       }
671.       osDisk: {
672.         createOption: 'FromImage'
673.         managedDisk: { storageAccountType: 'Standard_LRS' }
674.       }
675.     }
676.     networkProfile: {
677.       networkInterfaces: [{ id: jumpboxNic.id }]
678.     }
679.   }
680. }
681. 
682. // ============================================================================
683. // VPN Gateway P2S (for client remote access)
684. // ============================================================================
685. resource vpnGatewayPip 'Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses@2024-05-01' = if (deployVpnGateway) {
686.   name: 'pip-vpn-gateway'
687.   location: location
688.   sku: { name: 'Standard' }
689.   properties: {
690.     publicIPAllocationMethod: 'Static'
691.   }
692. }
693. 
694. resource vpnGateway 'Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways@2024-05-01' = if (deployVpnGateway) {
695.   name: 'vpngw-foundry-test'
696.   location: location
697.   properties: {
698.     gatewayType: 'Vpn'
699.     vpnType: 'RouteBased'
700.     sku: { name: 'VpnGw1', tier: 'VpnGw1' }
701.     ipConfigurations: [
702.       {
703.         name: 'default'
704.         properties: {
705.           publicIPAddress: { id: vpnGatewayPip.id }
706.           subnet: { id: vnet.properties.subnets[4].id } // GatewaySubnet
707.         }
708.       }
709.     ]
710.     vpnClientConfiguration: {
711.       vpnClientAddressPool: {
712.         addressPrefixes: ['172.16.0.0/24']
713.       }
714.       vpnClientProtocols: ['OpenVPN']
715.       vpnAuthenticationTypes: ['AAD']
716.       aadTenant: 'https://login.microsoftonline.com/${subscription().tenantId}'
717.       aadAudience: 'c632b3df-fb67-4d84-bdcf-b95ad541b5c8'
718.       aadIssuer: 'https://sts.windows.net/${subscription().tenantId}/'
719.     }
720.   }
721. }
722. 
723. // Additional outputs for portal access
724. output bastionId string = deployBastion ? bastion.id : ''
725. output jumpboxPrivateIp string = deployBastion ? jumpboxNic.properties.ipConfigurations[0].properties.privateIPAddress : ''
726. output vpnGatewayId string = deployVpnGateway ? vpnGateway.id : ''
727. output vpnGatewayPublicIp string = deployVpnGateway ? vpnGatewayPip.properties.ipAddress : ''
728.
```

C:\repos\FoundryVnet\foundry-vnet-test\scripts\01-deploy-foundry.ps1

<details>
<summary>74 lines</summary>

```
1. <#
2. .SYNOPSIS
3.     Deploy Foundry BYO VNet infrastructure via Bicep.
4. .DESCRIPTION
5.     Deploys main.bicep template with all Foundry resources, BYO VNet, PE, DNS, RBAC, Bastion, VPN.
6. #>
7. param(
8.     [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)]
9.     [string]$ConfigPath = "$PSScriptRoot\..\config\test-config.json",
10.     [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)]
11.     [switch]$WhatIf
12. )
13. 
14. $ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
15. $config = Get-Content $ConfigPath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
16. $rg = $config.resource_group
17. $location = $config.location
18. $bicepDir = "$PSScriptRoot\..\bicep"
19. 
20. Write-Host "`n========================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
21. Write-Host "  FAZA 1: DEPLOY FOUNDRY BYO VNet" -ForegroundColor Cyan
22. Write-Host "========================================`n" -ForegroundColor Cyan
23. 
24. # Validate VM password
25. if (-not $env:VM_ADMIN_PASSWORD) {
26.     Write-Host "ERROR: Set VM_ADMIN_PASSWORD env variable before deploying." -ForegroundColor Red
27.     Write-Host '  $env:VM_ADMIN_PASSWORD = "YourSecurePassword123!"' -ForegroundColor Yellow
28.     exit 1
29. }
30. 
31. # Ensure RG exists
32. Write-Host "Ensuring resource group $rg in $location..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
33. az group create --name $rg --location $location -o none 2>&1
34. 
35. $deployCmd = @(
36.     "az", "deployment", "group", "create",
37.     "--resource-group", $rg,
38.     "--template-file", "$bicepDir\main.bicep",
39.     "--parameters", "$bicepDir\main.bicepparam",
40.     "--name", "foundry-byovnet-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmmss')",
41.     "--verbose"
42. )
43. 
44. if ($WhatIf) {
45.     $deployCmd = @(
46.         "az", "deployment", "group", "validate",
47.         "--resource-group", $rg,
48.         "--template-file", "$bicepDir\main.bicep",
49.         "--parameters", "$bicepDir\main.bicepparam"
50.     )
51.     Write-Host "Running WHAT-IF validation only..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
52. }
53. 
54. Write-Host "Deploying Bicep template..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
55. Write-Host "  Template: $bicepDir\main.bicep" -ForegroundColor Gray
56. Write-Host "  RG: $rg | Region: $location" -ForegroundColor Gray
57. Write-Host "  This may take 15-30 minutes (VPN Gateway takes ~25 min)..." -ForegroundColor Gray
58. Write-Host ""
59. 
60. $result = & $deployCmd[0] $deployCmd[1..($deployCmd.Length-1)] 2>&1
61. $exitCode = $LASTEXITCODE
62. 
63. if ($exitCode -eq 0) {
64.     Write-Host "`n$([char]0x2705) Deployment SUCCEEDED" -ForegroundColor Green
65.     
66.     # Show key outputs
67.     Write-Host "`n--- Deployment Outputs ---" -ForegroundColor Cyan
68.     az deployment group show --resource-group $rg --name (az deployment group list --resource-group $rg --query "[0].name" -o tsv) --query "properties.outputs" -o json 2>&1
69. } else {
70.     Write-Host "`n$([char]0x274C) Deployment FAILED" -ForegroundColor Red
71.     Write-Host $result -ForegroundColor Red
72.     exit 1
73. }
74.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 56m 11s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\repos\FoundryVnet\foundry-vnet-test\scripts\04-test-network.ps1.**

C:\repos\FoundryVnet\foundry-vnet-test\scripts\04-test-network.ps1

<details>
<summary>125 lines</summary>

```
1. <#
2. .SYNOPSIS
3.     Test BYO VNet configuration: subnets, delegations, NSG, DNS zones.
4. #>
5. param(
6.     [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)]
7.     [string]$ConfigPath = "$PSScriptRoot\..\config\test-config.json"
8. )
9. 
10. $ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
11. $config = Get-Content $ConfigPath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
12. $rg = $config.resource_group
13. $vnetName = $config.vnet.name
14. 
15. $results = @()
16. $pass = 0; $fail = 0; $warn = 0
17. 
18. function Add-Result($test, $status, $detail) {
19.     $icon = switch ($status) { "PASS" { [char]0x2705 } "FAIL" { [char]0x274C } "WARN" { [char]0x26A0 } }
20.     $script:results += [PSCustomObject]@{ Icon=$icon; Status=$status; Test=$test; Detail=$detail }
21.     switch ($status) { "PASS" { $script:pass++ } "FAIL" { $script:fail++ } "WARN" { $script:warn++ } }
22. }
23. 
24. Write-Host "`n========================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
25. Write-Host "  FAZA 2: NETWORK VALIDATION (BYO VNet)" -ForegroundColor Cyan
26. Write-Host "========================================`n" -ForegroundColor Cyan
27. 
28. # --- 1. VNet existence ---
29. Write-Host "Checking VNet $vnetName..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
30. try {
31.     $vnet = az network vnet show --name $vnetName --resource-group $rg -o json 2>&1 | ConvertFrom-Json
32.     Add-Result "VNet: $vnetName" "PASS" "Address space: $($vnet.addressSpace.addressPrefixes -join ', ')"
33. } catch {
34.     Add-Result "VNet: $vnetName" "FAIL" "Not found in $rg"
35. }
36. 
37. # --- 2. Agent Subnet (delegated) ---
38. Write-Host "Checking Agent Subnet..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
39. $agentSubnet = $config.vnet.agent_subnet.name
40. try {
41.     $subnet = az network vnet subnet show --vnet-name $vnetName --name $agentSubnet --resource-group $rg -o json 2>&1 | ConvertFrom-Json
42.     Add-Result "Agent Subnet: $agentSubnet" "PASS" "CIDR: $($subnet.addressPrefix)"
43. 
44.     $delegations = $subnet.delegations | ForEach-Object { $_.serviceName }
45.     if ($delegations -contains "Microsoft.App/environments") {
46.         Add-Result "Agent Subnet Delegation" "PASS" "Microsoft.App/environments"
47.     } else {
48.         Add-Result "Agent Subnet Delegation" "FAIL" "Expected Microsoft.App/environments, got: $($delegations -join ', ')"
49.     }
50. } catch {
51.     Add-Result "Agent Subnet: $agentSubnet" "FAIL" "Not found"
52. }
53. 
54. # --- 3. PE Subnet (no delegation) ---
55. Write-Host "Checking PE Subnet..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
56. $peSubnet = $config.vnet.pe_subnet.name
57. try {
58.     $subnet = az network vnet subnet show --vnet-name $vnetName --name $peSubnet --resource-group $rg -o json 2>&1 | ConvertFrom-Json
59.     Add-Result "PE Subnet: $peSubnet" "PASS" "CIDR: $($subnet.addressPrefix)"
60. 
61.     if ($subnet.delegations.Count -eq 0) {
62.         Add-Result "PE Subnet No Delegation" "PASS" "No delegation (correct)"
63.     } else {
64.         Add-Result "PE Subnet No Delegation" "FAIL" "Has delegation: $($subnet.delegations[0].serviceName) — PE subnet must NOT have delegation"
65.     }
66. } catch {
67.     Add-Result "PE Subnet: $peSubnet" "FAIL" "Not found"
68. }
69. 
70. # --- 4. Bastion Subnet ---
71. Write-Host "Checking AzureBastionSubnet..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
72. try {
73.     $subnet = az network vnet subnet show --vnet-name $vnetName --name "AzureBastionSubnet" --resource-group $rg -o json 2>&1 | ConvertFrom-Json
74.     Add-Result "AzureBastionSubnet" "PASS" "CIDR: $($subnet.addressPrefix)"
75. } catch {
76.     Add-Result "AzureBastionSubnet" "WARN" "Not found (Bastion access won't work)"
77. }
78. 
79. # --- 5. GatewaySubnet ---
80. Write-Host "Checking GatewaySubnet..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
81. try {
82.     $subnet = az network vnet subnet show --vnet-name $vnetName --name "GatewaySubnet" --resource-group $rg -o json 2>&1 | ConvertFrom-Json
83.     Add-Result "GatewaySubnet" "PASS" "CIDR: $($subnet.addressPrefix)"
84. } catch {
85.     Add-Result "GatewaySubnet" "WARN" "Not found (VPN P2S won't work)"
86. }
87. 
88. # --- 6. Private DNS Zones linked to VNet ---
89. Write-Host "Checking Private DNS Zones..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
90. foreach ($zone in $config.dns_zones) {
91.     try {
92.         $links = az network private-dns link vnet list --zone-name $zone --resource-group $rg -o json 2>&1 | ConvertFrom-Json
93.         $linkedToVnet = $links | Where-Object { $_.virtualNetwork.id -like "*$vnetName*" }
94.         if ($linkedToVnet) {
95.             Add-Result "DNS Zone: $zone" "PASS" "Linked to $vnetName"
96.         } else {
97.             Add-Result "DNS Zone: $zone" "FAIL" "Exists but NOT linked to $vnetName"
98.         }
99.     } catch {
100.         Add-Result "DNS Zone: $zone" "FAIL" "Not found in $rg"
101.     }
102. }
103. 
104. # --- 7. Region consistency ---
105. Write-Host "Checking region consistency..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
106. $vnetLocation = $vnet.location
107. $expectedLocation = $config.location
108. if ($vnetLocation -eq $expectedLocation) {
109.     Add-Result "Region Consistency" "PASS" "VNet and config both in $expectedLocation"
110. } else {
111.     Add-Result "Region Consistency" "FAIL" "VNet in $vnetLocation, config expects $expectedLocation"
112. }
113. 
114. # --- Print results ---
115. Write-Host "`n========================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
116. Write-Host "  NETWORK VALIDATION RESULTS" -ForegroundColor Cyan
117. Write-Host "========================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
118. foreach ($r in $results) {
119.     $color = switch ($r.Status) { "PASS" { "Green" } "FAIL" { "Red" } "WARN" { "Yellow" } }
120.     Write-Host "$($r.Icon) $($r.Status)  $($r.Test): $($r.Detail)" -ForegroundColor $color
121. }
122. Write-Host "`n--- Summary: $pass PASS, $fail FAIL, $warn WARN ---" -ForegroundColor Cyan
123. 
124. if ($fail -gt 0) { exit 1 } else { exit 0 }
125.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 67m 35s</sub>

### ✅ `read_agent`

```
Agent completed. agent_id: validate-apim-files, agent_type: rubber-duck, status: completed, description: Validate APIM Bicep and scripts, elapsed: 264s, total_turns: 0, model: gpt-5.4, duration: 260s

Here’s the critical review.

## Blocking Issues

1. **`03-import-openapi-mcp.ps1` builds an invalid backend URL**
   - **Where:** `03-import-openapi-mcp.ps1:34-38`
   - **Impact:** `az cognitiveservices account list` typically returns a full endpoint URL already. Prefixing `https://` again produces `https://https://...`, so both APIM policies can point at a broken backend.
   - **Severity:** Blocking
   - **Fix:** Use `"$($aiAccount.properties.endpoint.TrimEnd('/'))"` directly, or normalize only when no scheme exists.

2. **The Agent proxy is wired to the wrong endpoint, wrong audience, and incomplete RBAC**
   - **Where:** `03-import-openapi-mcp.ps1:33-39, 115-167, 224-245`; `apim.bicep:219-231`
   - **Impact:** Foundry agent APIs are project-based and use the Foundry project endpoint (`https://<resource>.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/<project>`), not the Cognitive Services account endpoint. They also use a different Entra audience than OpenAI. Current config points agent traffic to `*.cognitiveservices.azure.com` with `authentication-managed-identity resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com/"`, so agent calls won’t authenticate or route correctly. The current RBAC assignment (`Cognitive Services OpenAI User`) is aligned to OpenAI-style inference, not Foundry project APIs.
   - **Severity:** Blocking
   - **Fix:** For the agent API, target the Foundry project endpoint, use the correct Entra audience for Foundry project APIs, and grant APIM MI the required Foundry role (at least **Azure AI User** at the right Foundry scope).

3. **`06-test-agent.ps1` does not actually test Agent Service data-plane isolation**
   - **Where:** `06-test-agent.ps1:142-175, 187-193`
   - **Impact:** The “negative test” calls `/openai/deployments/.../chat/completions` on the account endpoint. That validates OpenAI inference isolation, not Foundry Agent Service isolation. So this script can pass while the actual agent endpoint is broken.
   - **Severity:** Blocking
   - **Fix:** Add a real data-plane test against the Foundry project endpoint / agent runtime API, and update jumpbox instructions to exercise that same endpoint.

4. **Subnet ownership is split in a way that can delete `snet-apim` on later `main.bicep` redeploys**
   - **Where:** `main.bicep:64-112`; `apim.bicep:124-133`
   - **Impact:** `apim.bicep` adds `snet-apim` correctly as a child subnet, so it won’t remove existing subnets during that deployment. But `main.bicep` owns the VNet with an inline `subnets` array that omits `snet-apim`. A later redeploy of `main.bicep` can reconcile the VNet back to those 5 subnets and remove the APIM subnet, breaking APIM.
   - **Severity:** Blocking
   - **Fix:** Put all subnets under one ownership model. Best fix: manage subnets as child resources consistently, or include `snet-apim` in `main.bicep`.

## Non-Blocking Issues

1. **APIM NSG rules are not complete vs documented minimums, and tests only validate one inbound rule**
   - **Where:** `apim.bicep:45-119`; `07-test-apim.ps1:73-87`
   - **Impact:** Docs for internal VNet APIM call out additional required/minimum flows beyond what’s explicitly modeled here (for example inbound `6390`, outbound `80` to Internet, outbound to AAD, Key Vault, Azure Monitor, etc.). Today you may get away with Azure default NSG rules, but this template is not truly “complete,” and the test script won’t catch regressions.
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Fix:** Either explicitly model the documented minimum rules or state you rely on default NSG rules. Expand `07-test-apim.ps1` to validate `6390` and critical outbound dependencies.

2. **DNS validation is too shallow**
   - **Where:** `07-test-apim.ps1:113-137`
   - **Impact:** The template creates A records for gateway/developer/management/scm, but the test only checks the gateway record. Missing developer/management/scm records won’t be caught. Also, Microsoft docs list `portal` as a default hostname too; the template doesn’t create it.
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Fix:** Validate all expected records, and decide explicitly whether `portal` should also exist for your scenario.

3. **`03-import-openapi-mcp.ps1` doesn’t fail hard on partial import/policy failures**
   - **Where:** `03-import-openapi-mcp.ps1:92-108, 172-188, 211-250`
   - **Impact:** The script prints failures but keeps going and can still end “successfully” from an automation perspective.
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Fix:** Track failures and `exit 1` if any import/policy step fails.

4. **`02-deploy-apim.ps1 -WhatIf` is not actually What-If**
   - **Where:** `02-deploy-apim.ps1:51-59`
   - **Impact:** `validate` checks syntax/shape, not actual change impact. That’s misleading for a destructive networking deployment.
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Fix:** Use `az deployment group what-if` or rename the switch to `-ValidateOnly`.

## Suggestions

1. **`privateIPAddresses[0]` looks acceptable, but treat it as runtime-only**
   - **Where:** `apim.bicep:184-215, 238`
   - **Note:** This compiles to an ARM `reference(...)` call, so it should resolve after APIM provisioning. I don’t see a blocking issue here. Still, because APIM provisioning is long-running and IPs are runtime-assigned, keep a post-deploy validation step.

2. **OpenAI frontend path is awkward**
   - **Where:** `03-import-openapi-mcp.ps1:55`, `07-test-apim.ps1:184`
   - **Impact:** APIM path `openai` plus spec path `/openai/...` yields `/openai/openai/...`.
   - **Severity:** Suggestion
   - **Fix:** Either set APIM path to empty/shorter, or make the imported spec start at `/deployments/...`.

3. **Resource discovery by `[0]` is brittle**
   - **Where:** `02/03/06/07` scripts
   - **Impact:** If the RG ever contains multiple APIM or Cognitive Services resources, tests can bind to the wrong instance.
   - **Severity:** Suggestion
   - **Fix:** Resolve by deterministic name from config (`apim-foundry-test-${uniqueString(rgId)}`, `ai-foundry-byovnet-${uniqueString(rgId)}`) instead of “first resource in RG”.

Overall: the biggest real risks are the **agent proxy wiring**, the **fake agent isolation test**, and the **subnet ownership split**. Those are the items I would fix first.
```

</details>

130.                 requestBody = @{
131.                     content = @{
132.                         "application/json" = @{
133.                             schema = @{ type = "object" }
134.                         }
135.                     }
136.                 }
137.                 responses = @{ "200" = @{ description = "Agent created" } }
138.             }
139.             get = @{
140.                 operationId = "listAgents"
141.                 summary = "List agents"
142.                 parameters = @(
143.                     @{ name = "api-version"; "in" = "query"; required = $true; schema = @{ type = "string"; default = "2025-05-01-preview" } }
144.                 )
145.                 responses = @{ "200" = @{ description = "Agent list" } }
146.             }
147.         }
148.         "/agents/{agent-id}/threads" = @{
149.             post = @{
150.                 operationId = "createThread"
151.                 summary = "Create a thread for agent conversation"
152.                 parameters = @(
153.                     @{ name = "agent-id"; "in" = "path"; required = $true; schema = @{ type = "string" } }
154.                     @{ name = "api-version"; "in" = "query"; required = $true; schema = @{ type = "string"; default = "2025-05-01-preview" } }
155.                 )
156.                 requestBody = @{
157.                     content = @{
158.                         "application/json" = @{
159.                             schema = @{ type = "object" }
160.                         }
161.                     }
162.                 }
163.                 responses = @{ "200" = @{ description = "Thread created" } }
164.             }
165.         }
166.     }
167. } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 15
168. 
169. $agentSpecFile = "$env:TEMP\agent-spec.json"
170. $agentSpec | Out-File -FilePath $agentSpecFile -Encoding utf8 -Force
171. 
172. az apim api import `
173.     --api-id "foundry-agent" `
174.     --path "agent" `
175.     --service-name $apimName `
176.     --resource-group $rg `
177.     --specification-format OpenApi `
178.     --specification-path $agentSpecFile `
179.     --display-name "Foundry Agent Proxy (MCP)" `
180.     --protocols https `
181.     --subscription-required $false `
182.     -o none 2>&1
183. 
184. if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
185.     Write-Host "  $([char]0x2705) Agent API imported" -ForegroundColor Green
186. } else {
187.     Write-Host "  $([char]0x274C) Agent API import failed" -ForegroundColor Red
188. }
189. 
190. # ============================================================================
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 70m 44s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\repos\FoundryVnet\foundry-vnet-test\scripts\03-import-openapi-mcp.ps1.**

C:\repos\FoundryVnet\foundry-vnet-test\scripts\03-import-openapi-mcp.ps1 (lines 190-260)

<details>
<summary>71 lines</summary>

```
190. # ============================================================================
191. # 3. Set inbound policies — MI auth + backend URL
192. # ============================================================================
193. Write-Host "`n--- Configuring MI-authenticated backend policies ---" -ForegroundColor Yellow
194. 
195. $openaiPolicy = @"
196. <policies>
197.     <inbound>
198.         <base />
199.         <set-backend-service base-url="$foundryEndpoint" />
200.         <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com/" />
201.     </inbound>
202.     <backend><base /></backend>
203.     <outbound><base /></outbound>
204.     <on-error><base /></on-error>
205. </policies>
206. "@
207. 
208. $policyFile = "$env:TEMP\apim-policy-openai.xml"
209. $openaiPolicy | Out-File -FilePath $policyFile -Encoding utf8 -Force
210. 
211. az apim api policy create `
212.     --api-id "openai-chat" `
213.     --service-name $apimName `
214.     --resource-group $rg `
215.     --xml-file $policyFile `
216.     -o none 2>&1
217. 
218. if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
219.     Write-Host "  $([char]0x2705) OpenAI API policy configured (MI auth)" -ForegroundColor Green
220. } else {
221.     Write-Host "  $([char]0x274C) OpenAI API policy failed" -ForegroundColor Red
222. }
223. 
224. $agentPolicy = @"
225. <policies>
226.     <inbound>
227.         <base />
228.         <set-backend-service base-url="$foundryEndpoint" />
229.         <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com/" />
230.     </inbound>
231.     <backend><base /></backend>
232.     <outbound><base /></outbound>
233.     <on-error><base /></on-error>
234. </policies>
235. "@
236. 
237. $agentPolicyFile = "$env:TEMP\apim-policy-agent.xml"
238. $agentPolicy | Out-File -FilePath $agentPolicyFile -Encoding utf8 -Force
239. 
240. az apim api policy create `
241.     --api-id "foundry-agent" `
242.     --service-name $apimName `
243.     --resource-group $rg `
244.     --xml-file $agentPolicyFile `
245.     -o none 2>&1
246. 
247. if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
248.     Write-Host "  $([char]0x2705) Agent API policy configured (MI auth)" -ForegroundColor Green
249. } else {
250.     Write-Host "  $([char]0x274C) Agent API policy failed" -ForegroundColor Red
251. }
252. 
253. # ============================================================================
254. # Cleanup temp files
255. # ============================================================================
256. Remove-Item -Path $specFile, $agentSpecFile, $policyFile, $agentPolicyFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
257. 
258. # ============================================================================
259. # Summary
260. # ============================================================================
```

</details>

### 💬 Copilot

Gotowe! Teraz masz **jeden skrypt** `run-all.ps1` który odpala wszystko:

```powershell
# Pełny test (deploy + testy) — ~45-60 min
.\run-all.ps1

# Tylko walidacja (bez deploy) — jeśli infrastruktura już istnieje
.\run-all.ps1 -SkipDeploy

# Bez APIM (tylko Foundry + VNet)
.\run-all.ps1 -SkipApim

# Dry-run (sprawdza Bicep ale nie deploy)
.\run-all.ps1 -WhatIf
```

Kolejność: Preflight → Deploy Foundry (15-30 min) → Deploy APIM (30-45 min) → Import APIs → Testy (Network, PE, Agent, Identity, Portal, APIM) → Raport GO/NO-GO.

---

<sub>⏱️ 104m 40s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\repos\FoundryVnet\foundry-vnet-test\scripts\01-deploy-foundry.ps1.**

C:\repos\FoundryVnet\foundry-vnet-test\scripts\01-deploy-foundry.ps1

<details>
<summary>74 lines</summary>

```
1. <#
2. .SYNOPSIS
3.     Deploy Foundry BYO VNet infrastructure via Bicep.
4. .DESCRIPTION
5.     Deploys main.bicep template with all Foundry resources, BYO VNet, PE, DNS, RBAC, Bastion, VPN.
6. #>
7. param(
8.     [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)]
9.     [string]$ConfigPath = "$PSScriptRoot\..\config\test-config.json",
10.     [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)]
11.     [switch]$WhatIf
12. )
13. 
14. $ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
15. $config = Get-Content $ConfigPath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
16. $rg = $config.resource_group
17. $location = $config.location
18. $bicepDir = "$PSScriptRoot\..\bicep"
19. 
20. Write-Host "`n========================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
21. Write-Host "  FAZA 1: DEPLOY FOUNDRY BYO VNet" -ForegroundColor Cyan
22. Write-Host "========================================`n" -ForegroundColor Cyan
23. 
24. # Validate VM password
25. if (-not $env:VM_ADMIN_PASSWORD) {
26.     Write-Host "ERROR: Set VM_ADMIN_PASSWORD env variable before deploying." -ForegroundColor Red
27.     Write-Host '  $env:VM_ADMIN_PASSWORD = "YourSecurePassword123!"' -ForegroundColor Yellow
28.     exit 1
29. }
30. 
31. # Ensure RG exists
32. Write-Host "Ensuring resource group $rg in $location..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
33. az group create --name $rg --location $location -o none 2>&1
34. 
35. $deployCmd = @(
36.     "az", "deployment", "group", "create",
37.     "--resource-group", $rg,
38.     "--template-file", "$bicepDir\main.bicep",
39.     "--parameters", "$bicepDir\main.bicepparam",
40.     "--name", "foundry-byovnet-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmmss')",
41.     "--verbose"
42. )
43. 
44. if ($WhatIf) {
45.     $deployCmd = @(
46.         "az", "deployment", "group", "validate",
47.         "--resource-group", $rg,
48.         "--template-file", "$bicepDir\main.bicep",
49.         "--parameters", "$bicepDir\main.bicepparam"
50.     )
51.     Write-Host "Running WHAT-IF validation only..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
52. }
53. 
54. Write-Host "Deploying Bicep template..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
55. Write-Host "  Template: $bicepDir\main.bicep" -ForegroundColor Gray
56. Write-Host "  RG: $rg | Region: $location" -ForegroundColor Gray
57. Write-Host "  This may take 15-30 minutes (VPN Gateway takes ~25 min)..." -ForegroundColor Gray
58. Write-Host ""
59. 
60. $result = & $deployCmd[0] $deployCmd[1..($deployCmd.Length-1)] 2>&1
61. $exitCode = $LASTEXITCODE
62. 
63. if ($exitCode -eq 0) {
64.     Write-Host "`n$([char]0x2705) Deployment SUCCEEDED" -ForegroundColor Green
65.     
66.     # Show key outputs
67.     Write-Host "`n--- Deployment Outputs ---" -ForegroundColor Cyan
68.     az deployment group show --resource-group $rg --name (az deployment group list --resource-group $rg --query "[0].name" -o tsv) --query "properties.outputs" -o json 2>&1
69. } else {
70.     Write-Host "`n$([char]0x274C) Deployment FAILED" -ForegroundColor Red
71.     Write-Host $result -ForegroundColor Red
72.     exit 1
73. }
74.
```

</details>

{
  "command": "$rg = \"rg-foundry-byovnet\"\n$sub = \"38b8b5d4-b3a1-4892-b2a7-3f5bcfdad59f\"\n$projectMI = \"836b9920-537f-4fa3-a95e-2942e7eca04a\"\n$accountMI = \"f1c3c13f-8f90-4ee6-a2dc-95c736548e5e\"\n$storageId = \"/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/stfoundryunrkke4ibdxcm\"\n$searchId = \"/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.Search/searchServices/srch-foundry-unrkke4ibdxcm\"\n$cosmosId = \"/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.DocumentDB/databaseAccounts/cosmos-foundry-unrkke4ibdxcm\"\n\n$roles = @(\n    # Project MI → Storage\n    @{scope=$storageId; role=\"b7e6dc6d-f1e8-4753-8033-0f276bb0955b\"; principal=$projectMI; desc=\"Storage Blob Data Owner\"},\n    @{scope=$storageId; role=\"17d1049b-9a84-46fb-8f53-869881c3d3ab\"; principal=$projectMI; desc=\"Storage Account Contributor\"},\n    @{scope=$storageId; role=\"974c5e8b-45b9-4653-ba55-5f855dd0fb88\"; principal=$projectMI; desc=\"Storage Queue Data Contributor\"},\n    # Project MI → Search\n    @{scope=$searchId; role=\"8ebe5a00-799e-43f5-93ac-243d3dce84a7\"; principal=$projectMI; desc=\"Search Index Data Contributor\"},\n    @{scope=$searchId; role=\"7ca78c08-252a-4471-8644-bb5ff32d4ba0\"; principal=$projectMI; desc=\"Search Service Contributor\"},\n    # Project MI → CosmosDB\n    @{scope=$cosmosId; role=\"230815da-be43-4aae-9cb4-875f7bd000aa\"; principal=$projectMI; desc=\"Cosmos DB Operator\"},\n    # Account MI → Storage\n    @{scope=$storageId; role=\"b7e6dc6d-f1e8-4753-8033-0f276bb0955b\"; principal=$accountMI; desc=\"Storage Blob Data Owner (Account)\"},\n    @{scope=$storageId; role=\"974c5e8b-45b9-4653-ba55-5f855dd0fb88\"; principal=$accountMI; desc=\"Storage Queue Data Contributor (Account)\"},\n    # Account MI → Search\n    @{scope=$searchId; role=\"8ebe5a00-799e-43f5-93ac-243d3dce84a7\"; principal=$accountMI; desc=\"Search Index Data Contributor (Account)\"}\n)\n\n$ok = 0; $skip = 0; $fail = 0\nforeach ($r in $roles) {\n    $out = az role assignment create --assignee-object-id $r.principal --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal --role $r.role --scope $r.scope -o none 2>&1\n    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { $ok++; Write-Host \"  ✅ $($r.desc)\" -ForegroundColor Green }\n    elseif ($out -match \"already exists\") { $skip++; Write-Host \"  ⏭️ $($r.desc) (exists)\" -ForegroundColor Gray }\n    else { $fail++; Write-Host \"  ❌ $($r.desc): $out\" -ForegroundColor Red }\n}\nWrite-Host \"`nRBAC: $ok created, $skip existing, $fail failed\"",
  "description": "Create RBAC role assignments",
  "initial_wait": 30
}

Fix storage connection: AzureBlob → AzureStorageAccount\nWrite-Host \"Fixing storage connection category...\"\n$body = @{\n    properties = @{\n        category = \"AzureStorageAccount\"\n        authType = \"AAD\"\n        target = \"https://$storageAcct.blob.core.windows.net\"\n        metadata = @{\n            ResourceId = $storageId\n            AccountName = $storageAcct\n            ContainerName = \"default\"\n        }\n    }\n} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5\n\naz rest --method PUT `\n  --url \"https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/$aiName/projects/$projectName/connections/connection-storage?api-version=2025-04-01-preview\" `\n  --body $body `\n  -o none 2>&1\nWrite-Host \"Storage connection fix exit: $LASTEXITCODE\"",
  "description": "Fix storage connection category",
  "initial_wait": 15
}

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "$sub = \"38b8b5d4-b3a1-4892-b2a7-3f5bcfdad59f\"\n$rg = \"rg-foundry-byovnet\"\n$base = \"https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/ai-foundry-byovnet-unrkke4ibdxcm/projects/project-agent-test/connections\"\n\n# Delete old connection\nWrite-Host \"Deleting old storage connection...\"\naz rest --method DELETE --url \"$base/connection-storage?api-version=2025-04-01-preview\" -o none 2>&1\nWrite-Host \"Delete exit: $LASTEXITCODE\"\n\n# Recreate with correct category\nWrite-Host \"Creating with AzureStorageAccount category...\"\n$storageId = \"/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/stfoundryunrkke4ibdxcm\"\n\n# Write body to temp file to avoid escaping issues\n$bodyObj = @{\n    properties = @{\n        category = \"AzureStorageAccount\"\n        authType = \"AAD\"\n        target = \"https://stfoundryunrkke4ibdxcm.blob.core.windows.net\"\n        metadata = @{\n            ResourceId = $storageId\n            AccountName = \"stfoundryunrkke4ibdxcm\"\n            ContainerName = \"default\"\n        }\n    }\n}\n$bodyObj | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5 | Out-File -FilePath \"$env:TEMP\\conn-body.json\" -Encoding utf8\n\naz rest --method PUT `\n  --url \"$base/connection-storage?api-version=2025-04-01-preview\" `\n  --body \"@$env:TEMP\\conn-body.json\" `\n  -o none 2>&1\nWrite-Host \"Create exit: $LASTEXITCODE\"",
  "description": "Recreate storage connection with correct type",
  "initial_wait": 15
}
```

<sub>⏱️ 126m 4s</sub>

### ✅ `powershell`

**Create capability host**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "$sub = \"38b8b5d4-b3a1-4892-b2a7-3f5bcfdad59f\"\n$rg = \"rg-foundry-byovnet\"\n$base = \"https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/ai-foundry-byovnet-unrkke4ibdxcm/projects/project-agent-test\"\n\n# Verify storage connection\n$cat = az rest --method GET --url \"$base/connections/connection-storage?api-version=2025-04-01-preview\" --query \"properties.category\" -o tsv 2>&1\nWrite-Host \"Storage connection category: $cat\"\n\n# Create capability host\nWrite-Host \"`nCreating capability host...\"\n$capBody = @{\n    properties = @{\n        capabilityHostKind = \"Agents\"\n        vectorStoreConnections = @(\"connection-search\")\n        storageConnections = @(\"connection-storage\")\n        threadStorageConnections = @(\"connection-cosmos\")\n    }\n} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5\n$capBody | Out-File -FilePath \"$env:TEMP\\cap-body.json\" -Encoding utf8\n\naz rest --method PUT `\n  --url \"$base/capabilityHosts/default?api-version=2025-04-01-preview\" `\n  --body \"@$env:TEMP\\cap-body.json\" `\n  2>&1\nWrite-Host \"`nCapability Host exit: $LASTEXITCODE\"",
  "description": "Create capability host",
  "initial_wait": 30
}
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "$sub = \"38b8b5d4-b3a1-4892-b2a7-3f5bcfdad59f\"\n$rg = \"rg-foundry-byovnet\"\n$url = \"https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/ai-foundry-byovnet-unrkke4ibdxcm/projects/project-agent-test/capabilityHosts/default?api-version=2025-04-01-preview\"\n\n$wait = 0\ndo {\n    Start-Sleep -Seconds 15\n    $wait += 15\n    $state = az rest --method GET --url $url --query \"properties.provisioningState\" -o tsv 2>&1\n    Write-Host \"[$wait`s] Capability Host state: $state\"\n} while ($state -notin @(\"Succeeded\",\"Failed\") -and $wait -lt 300)\n\nif ($state -eq \"Succeeded\") { Write-Host \"`n✅ Capability Host READY!\" -ForegroundColor Green }\nelse { Write-Host \"`n❌ Capability Host: $state\" -ForegroundColor Red }",
  "description": "Wait for capability host provisioning",
  "initial_wait": 60
}
```

C:\repos\FoundryVnet\foundry-vnet-test\scripts\08-test-identity.ps1 (lines 1-30)

<details>
<summary>30 lines</summary>

```
1. <#
2. .SYNOPSIS
3.     Test Managed Identity and RBAC role assignments.
4. #>
5. param(
6.     [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)]
7.     [string]$ConfigPath = "$PSScriptRoot\..\config\test-config.json"
8. )
9. 
10. $ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
11. $config = Get-Content $ConfigPath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
12. $rg = $config.resource_group
13. 
14. $results = @()
15. $pass = 0; $fail = 0; $warn = 0
16. 
17. function Add-Result($test, $status, $detail) {
18.     $icon = switch ($status) { "PASS" { [char]0x2705 } "FAIL" { [char]0x274C } "WARN" { [char]0x26A0 } }
19.     $script:results += [PSCustomObject]@{ Icon=$icon; Status=$status; Test=$test; Detail=$detail }
20.     switch ($status) { "PASS" { $script:pass++ } "FAIL" { $script:fail++ } "WARN" { $script:warn++ } }
21. }
22. 
23. Write-Host "`n========================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
24. Write-Host "  FAZA 5: IDENTITY & RBAC VALIDATION" -ForegroundColor Cyan
25. Write-Host "========================================`n" -ForegroundColor Cyan
26. 
27. # --- 1. Foundry Account MI ---
28. Write-Host "Checking Foundry Account Managed Identity..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
29. $aiAccounts = az cognitiveservices account list --resource-group $rg -o json 2>&1 | ConvertFrom-Json
30. $mainAccount = $aiAccounts | Where-Object { $_.kind -eq "AIServices" } | Select-Object -First 1
```

99. try {
100.     $vmStatus = az vm get-instance-view --name "vm-jumpbox" --resource-group $rg --query "instanceView.statuses[1].displayStatus" -o tsv 2>&1
101.     Add-Result "Portal" "Jumpbox VM" $(if($vmStatus.Trim() -eq "VM running"){"PASS"}else{"WARN"}) $vmStatus.Trim()
102. } catch {
103.     Add-Result "Portal" "Jumpbox VM" "WARN" "Not deployed"
104. }
105. 
106. try {
107.     $vpnGw = az network vnet-gateway show --name "vpngw-foundry-test" --resource-group $rg -o json 2>&1 | ConvertFrom-Json
108.     Add-Result "Portal" "VPN Gateway" $(if($vpnGw.provisioningState -eq "Succeeded"){"PASS"}else{"FAIL"}) $vpnGw.provisioningState
109. } catch {
110.     Add-Result "Portal" "VPN Gateway" "WARN" "Not deployed"
111. }
112.
```

</details>

Assign Required RBAC Roles:**\n\nYou need to be assigned one of the roles that grants permissions to build agents. Assign Role at the Correct Scope:**\n- The role must be assigned to your **user account**, **group**, or **service principal** at the **resource group**, **subscription**, or specific **AI Foundry project** level.\n\n**3. How to Assign the Role:**\nIf you have the required access to do so, or can request it from your Azure administrator:\n1. Go to the **Azure Portal** ([https://portal.azure.com](https://portal.azure.com)).\n2. Navigate to the **Resource Group** or **AI Foundry project** where the portal is provisioned.\n3. Select **Access control (IAM)** from the left menu.\n4. Click **+ Add > Add role assignment**.\n5. Select the necessary role (**Cognitive Services Contributor**, **Contributor**, or **Owner**).\n6. Add your Azure account (or group) as a member.\n7. Click **Save**.\n\n**4. Refresh the Portal:**\n- After the role is assigned, wait a few minutes, then refresh or sign out and back in to the AI Foundry portal.\n\n**5. Confirm Your Permissions:**\nYou should now have permissions to **build agents**. If not, check whether the role was assigned at the correct scope and that your account type matches organizational policy.\n\n---\n\n### Notes\n- **You need appropriate Azure Active Directory permissions** to assign roles. If you don't, contact your IT admin or Azure subscription owner.\n- RBAC can take a few minutes to propagate.\n\nWould you like a **step-by-step screenshot guide** or a **sample Azure CLI/PowerShell command** for assigning the role? Problem jasny! **Azure AI User** to rola **read-only** — nie pozwala budować agentów. Potrzebujesz **Azure AI Developer** + role na zasobach:

---

<sub>⏱️ 287m 46s</sub>

### ✅ `powershell`

**Assign portal roles to admin user**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "$rg = \"rg-foundry-byovnet\"\n$sub = \"38b8b5d4-b3a1-4892-b2a7-3f5bcfdad59f\"\n$userUpn = \"admin@MngEnvMCAP833611.onmicrosoft.com\"\n$aiAccountId = \"/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/ai-foundry-byovnet-unrkke4ibdxcm\"\n$projectId = \"$aiAccountId/projects/project-agent-test\"\n$storageId = \"/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/stfoundryunrkke4ibdxcm\"\n$searchId = \"/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.Search/searchServices/srch-foundry-unrkke4ibdxcm\"\n$cosmosId = \"/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.DocumentDB/databaseAccounts/cosmos-foundry-unrkke4ibdxcm\"\n\n$roles = @(\n    # Azure AI Developer on account (covers project too)\n    @{role=\"64702f94-c441-49e6-a78b-ef80e0188fee\"; scope=$aiAccountId; desc=\"Azure AI Developer (Account)\"},\n    # Cognitive Services OpenAI User on account\n    @{role=\"5e0bd9bd-7b93-4f28-af87-19fc36ad61bd\"; scope=$aiAccountId; desc=\"Cognitive Services OpenAI User\"},\n    # Storage Blob Data Contributor\n    @{role=\"ba92f5b4-2d11-453d-a403-e96b0029c9fe\"; scope=$storageId; desc=\"Storage Blob Data Contributor\"},\n    # Search Index Data Contributor\n    @{role=\"8ebe5a00-799e-43f5-93ac-243d3dce84a7\"; scope=$searchId; desc=\"Search Index Data Contributor\"},\n    # Cosmos DB Operator\n    @{role=\"230815da-be43-4aae-9cb4-875f7bd000aa\"; scope=$cosmosId; desc=\"Cosmos DB Operator\"}\n)\n\n$ok = 0\nforeach ($r in $roles) {\n    $out = az role assignment create --assignee $userUpn --role $r.role --scope $r.scope -o none 2>&1\n    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { $ok++; Write-Host \"✅ $($r.desc)\" -ForegroundColor Green }\n    else { Write-Host \"❌ $($r.desc): $out\" -ForegroundColor Red }\n}\nWrite-Host \"`nAssigned: $ok/$($roles.Count)\"",
  "description": "Assign portal roles to admin user",
  "initial_wait": 30
}
```
